quotes.text: Look, I have been hungover on every continent except Antarctica, and frankly that's just an incomplete bucket list. Don't applaud that. Or do. I don't care — you all came here voluntarily.
quotes.speaker: Christopher Hitchens
quotes.context: Audience Q&A session following his lecture 'Cheap Thrills and Expensive Regrets' at the 92nd Street Y, New York City, October 2007
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Hitchens openly celebrated drinking, traveled obsessively, and had a razor-dry audience rapport that made self-implicating asides feel like scholarship. The giveaway is the slightly too-tidy 'incomplete bucket list' framing — Hitchens would've gone messier and more specific.

quotes.text: I've read every bad review I've ever gotten. Every single one. Twice. And then I went and did the show anyway and the crowd lost their shit, so what does that tell you about critics? They're just sad people who can't afford good seats.
quotes.speaker: Don Rickles
quotes.context: Pre-show press gaggle at the Sands Hotel & Casino, Las Vegas, recorded for a segment on 'The Tonight Show with Johnny Carson,' aired November 1978
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Rickles built his entire persona on cheerfully contemptuous put-downs, and taking shots at critics while bragging about crowd reaction is completely on-brand. The giveaway is it's a bit too structurally clean — Rickles would've interrupted himself with a tangential insult.

quotes.text: The night I won the Grammy I went back to my hotel room, ordered room service at two in the morning — just a massive plate of fries and a bottle of something I can't spell — and sat there in my good dress eating with my hands. That was the real celebration. The after-party was full of assholes.
quotes.speaker: Amy Winehouse
quotes.context: Candid interview with NME journalist Paul Stokes conducted at the Chateau Marmont, Los Angeles, February 2008
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the down-to-earth, anti-glamour deflation fits Winehouse's personality perfectly, as does the specific sensory detail and the blunt 'full of assholes' kicker. The giveaway is it's almost *too* charming and self-aware — a cleaned-up version of how she actually rambled.

quotes.text: I'm in one mode right now: Entertain the shit out of people. And if people don't like it, I just don't give a fuck anymore. I just want to cut to the chase.
quotes.speaker: Bob Saget
quotes.context: In a Rolling Stone interview (republished from the vault), the 'America's Dad' comedian on his post-Full House, R-rated stand-up mindset.

source.title: Rolling Stone — A Bob Saget Interview From the 'Rolling Stone' Vault

quotes.text: There are 400,000 words in the English language, and there are seven of them that you can't say on television. What a ratio that is. 399,993 to seven. They must really be bad.
quotes.speaker: George Carlin
quotes.context: Carlin's landmark "Seven Words You Can Never Say on Television" routine from his 1972 album Class Clown, listing the broadcast-banned words verbatim and reveling in saying them all out loud.

source.title: The Big Seven Words — George Carlin

quotes.text: And cigarettes gave me the patience to sit and write those thoughts down in a comedy-friendly format that you could understand. And alcohol gives me the courage to stand up here in front of you judgmental pricks and do it with a quarter million dollars worth of cameras in your face.
quotes.speaker: Doug Stanhope
quotes.context: From Doug Stanhope's 2007 stand-up special "No Refunds" — his defiant, self-celebrating defense of lifelong drug, cigarette, and alcohol use as the engine of his comedy.

source.title: Doug Stanhope: No Refunds (2007) — Full Transcript
